name: Atsuta Shrine
shortDescription: Atsuta Shrine is a Shinto shrine traditionally believed to have been founded during the reign of Emperor Keikō (71-130 AD). It is one of the most important Shinto shrines in Japan, holding the sacred sword Kusanagi.
description: Atsuta Shrine (熱田神宮, Atsuta-jingū) is a Shinto shrine located in Atsuta-ku, Nagoya, Japan. It is revered as one of the most important shrines in Japan, second only to Ise Grand Shrine. The shrine is traditionally believed to have been founded during the reign of Emperor Keikō, but its origins are shrouded in legend. The most significant aspect of Atsuta Shrine is its possession of the Kusanagi-no-Tsurugi, a legendary sword that is one of the Three Imperial Regalia of Japan. According to tradition, the sword was originally found within the eight-headed serpent Yamata no Orochi by the god Susanoo. It was later gifted to Yamato Takeru, a legendary prince, who used it to defeat his enemies. The sword eventually found its way to Atsuta, where it has been enshrined ever since. The shrine complex includes numerous buildings, including the main hall (Honden), offering hall (Heiden), and other auxiliary structures. Over the centuries, Atsuta Shrine has been rebuilt and renovated many times, reflecting its importance as a major center of Shinto worship. The shrine plays a significant role in Japanese culture and history, representing a powerful symbol of imperial authority and national identity. While the Kusanagi is not publicly displayed, its presence is deeply felt within the shrine's atmosphere. The shrine attracts millions of visitors each year, offering prayers for good fortune, health, and prosperity.

location.cosmological: Located in Nagoya, Japan, considered a sacred space within the earthly realm.
